ground_truth.title: Project Management Tutorial
ground_truth.main_content: Smartsheet provides essential tools for effective project management to empower your teams to execute together with speed and accountability and reach successful outcomes faster. Follow these step-by-step instructions to modify project settings. Project settings are now sheet settings in table, board, and timeline views. You can access Project Settings from Gantt, grid, or table view. Use the Start date and End date columns to track the start and end dates. To use Gantt view, your sheet must have both a start date and an end date — and those dates can't be calculated with formulas. The following image shows a project's dependency settings. To enable dependencies, you must assign Predecessor and Duration columns. If your sheet doesn't already have these columns, Smartsheet automatically creates them for you. The Duration column automates scheduling task start and end dates. It represents the number of working days the task must take to complete. Follow these step-by-step instructions to create milestones in your project sheet. In the Gantt and timeline views, a diamond shape represents a milestone. You can create a milestone by setting the item's duration value to zero. Follow these step-by-step instructions to setup your parent rollup functionality in a project sheet. You can create parent rows by indenting other rows beneath them. These indented rows become child rows. The Start Date for a parent is the earliest start date from all of its child rows. The End Date is the latest end date from all its child rows. The Duration on the parent reflects the number of working days between the parent row's Start Date and End Date. The % Complete on the parent row calculates a weighted percentage based on the Duration and % Complete entered on each child row.
with: Smartsheet provides essential tools for effective project management to empower your teams to execute together with speed and accountability and reach successful outcomes faster.
To use Gantt view, your sheet must have both a start date and an end date — and those dates can't be calculated with formulas.
To enable dependencies, you must assign Predecessor and Duration columns.
In the Gantt and timeline views, a diamond shape represents a milestone.
The % Complete on the parent row calculates a weighted percentage based on the Duration and % Complete entered on each child row.
